boundary_reason: A repeatedly useful local solution can feel exceptionally valuable inside one domain while still remaining uncertain as a registry-wide exceptional-value case.
classification_rationale: This stays borderline because the exceptional-value pressure is real, but so is the concrete local usefulness that keeps the case from collapsing into obvious inflation.
why_not_likely_overvaluation: The solution is concrete, copy-ready and tied to a recurring operational problem rather than to a broad speculative claim.
why_not_justified_exception: The exceptional-value claim is still broader than the narrow bounded confidence pattern that normally makes an exception clean.
why_not_adjacent_classification: This is not a clean justified exception because an exceptional-value claim is stronger than narrow high-confidence groundedness. It is not clearly likely overvaluation because the solution is concrete, copy-ready and tied to a recurring operational problem.
bounded_reading: Read as a local high-signal solution whose exceptional value claim should stay reviewable rather than automatically normalized or automatically defended.

source_snapshot.overvaluation_signal: value is already at exceptional territory and therefore asks for review pressure
source_snapshot.defensibility_signal: the solution is copy-ready, repeatedly useful and locally grounded in one recurring domain problem
source_snapshot.review_anchor: Existing soft-flag review treats high local usefulness with near-exceptional value as a softer and more ambiguous zone than proposal-confidence inflation.
